sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: Fortinet은 2025년 3분기 매출 17억 2,490만 달러를 기록하며 견조한 실적을 보였으나, 공급망 제약과 시장 경쟁 심화라는 잠재적 리스크에 직면해 있습니다.

keyPoints.text: 보안 솔루션 시장 내 경쟁 심화로 인해 시장 점유율 유지 및 확대가 핵심 사업 동인으로 작용하고 있습니다.
클라우드 기반 및 AI 솔루션으로의 시장 변화에 대응하는 능력이 향후 성장과 서비스 수준 유지의 관건입니다.
채널 파트너를 통한 솔루션 판매 확대와 대형 엔터프라이즈 고객 대상 영업의 복잡성이 실적 변동성을 유발할 수 있습니다.

riskChanges.text: 공급망 제약 및 핵심 부품의 제한된 공급처로 인해 재고 관리와 운영 결과가 예측 불가능하게 변동될 위험이 있습니다.
제품 내 취약점 발생이나 보안 사고 대응 실패 시 평판 훼손 및 운영 결과에 심각한 타격을 입을 수 있습니다.

businessChanges.text: 과거 공급망 차질에 따른 재고 확보 전략으로 인해 매출 수준과 관계없이 부품 및 완제품 구매 비용을 부담해야 할 가능성이 존재합니다.
FortiGuard 보안 구독 및 FortiCare 기술 지원 서비스 매출에 대한 의존도가 높으며, 해당 부문의 실적 변동이 전체 운영 결과에 영향을 미칩니다.

financialHighlights.text: 2025년 3분기 매출 17억 2,490만 달러, 영업이익 5억 4,730만 달러를 달성하며 안정적인 수익성을 유지했습니다.
총자산 93억 6,130만 달러와 현금및현금성자산 19억 9,570만 달러를 보유하여 견고한 재무 상태를 확보했습니다.

watchItems.text: 매출의 상당 부분이 채널 파트너에 집중되어 있어, 파트너사와의 관계 및 영업 생산성 변화가 향후 매출 성장률의 선행 지표가 될 것입니다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: Quarter ended 2025-09-30 revenue = 17억 2,490만 달러 (USD 1.725B; 1,725 million), XBRL range 2025-07-01..2025-09-30. Readable Korean unit: 17억 2,500만 달러.
Quarter ended 2025-09-30 operating income = 5억 4,730만 달러 (USD 0.547B; 547 million), XBRL range 2025-07-01..2025-09-30. Readable Korean unit: 5억 4,700만 달러.
Quarter ended 2025-09-30 net income = 4억 7,390만 달러 (USD 0.474B; 474 million), XBRL range 2025-07-01..2025-09-30. Readable Korean unit: 4억 7,400만 달러.
Quarter ended 2025-09-30 total assets = 93억 6,130만 달러 (USD 9.361B; 9,361 million), XBRL range instant..2025-09-30. Readable Korean unit: 93억 6,100만 달러.
Quarter ended 2025-09-30 cash and cash equivalents = 19억 9,570만 달러 (USD 1.996B; 1,996 million), XBRL range instant..2025-09-30. Readable Korean unit: 19억 9,600만 달러.

We face intense competition in our market and we may not maintain or improve our competitive position. • We are susceptible to defects or vulnerabilities, including critical vulnerabilities, in our products or services, as well as reputational harm from the failure or misuse of our products or services, and any actual or perceived defects or vulnerabilities, including critical vulnerabilities, in our products or services, failure of our products or services to detect or prevent a security incident or to cause a disruption to operations, failure of our customers to implement preventative actions such as updates to one of our deployed solutions or failure to help secure our customers, could cause our products or services to allow unauthorized access to our customers’ networks and harm our operational results and reputation more significantly as compared to other companies. Our Product Secu

ts in our products come from limited sources of supply. • As a result of supply chain disruptions in previous periods, we increased our purchase order commitments in previous periods and were in some instances required to and may in the future be required to accept or pay for components and finished goods regardless of our level of sales in a particular period, which may negatively or unpredictably impact our operating results and financial condition. • Our billings, revenue, and free cash flow growth may slow or may not continue to grow, and our operating margins may decline. • Our real estate assets, including construction, acquisitions, improvements, leasing activity, and ongoing maintenance and management of office buildings, warehouses, data centers and points of presence (“PoPs”), as well as data center operations, expansions or enhancements, could involve significant risks to our

business. • Any weakness in sales strategy, productivity, personnel, retention and execution could negatively impact our results of operations. • We are dependent on the continued services and performance of our senior management, as well as our ability to hire, retain and motivate qualified personnel. • We rely on third-party channel partners for substantially all of our billings, revenue, and a small number of distributors represents a large percentage of our revenue and accounts receivable. • Reliance on a concentration of shipments at the end of the quarter or changes in shipping terms could cause our billings and revenue to fall below expected levels. • We rely significantly on revenue from FortiGuard and other security subscriptions and FortiCare technical support services, and revenue from these services may decline or fluctuate in manners that could adversely impact our results o

Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations In addition to historical information, this Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q contains forward-looking statements within the meaning of Section 27A of the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, and Section 21E of the Securities and Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “Exchange Act”). These statements include, among other things, statements concerning our expectations regarding: • continued growth and market share gains; • variability in sales in certain product and service categories from year to year and between quarters; • expected impact of sales from certain products and services; • increasing or decreasing inflation or stagflation, and changing interest rates in many geographies and changes in currency exchange rates and currency regulations; • competition in our markets; • macroeconomic, geopolitical f

actors and other disruption on our manufacturing or sales, including tariffs or other trade disruptions, public health issues, wars, natural disasters and economic growth; • government regulation and other policies; • drivers of long-term growth and operating leverage, such as pricing of our products and services, sales productivity, pipeline and capacity, functionality, value and technology improvements in our product and service offerings; • growing our solution sales through channel partners to businesses, service providers and government organizations, our ability to execute these sales and the complexity of providing solutions to all segments (including the increased competition and unpredictability of timing associated with sales to larger enterprises), the impact of sales to these organizations on our long-term growth, expansion and operating results, and the effectiveness of our

sales organization; • our ability to successfully anticipate market changes, including those related to cloud-based and AI solutions and to sell, support and meet service level agreements related to cloud-based solutions; • growth expectations for the secure networking market; • supply chain constraints, component availability and other factors affecting our manufacturing capacity, delivery, cost and inventory management; • forecasts of future demand and targeted inventory levels, including changing market drivers and demands; • the effect of backlog from current or prior quarters, including its effect on growth of in-quarter billings and revenue; • our ability to hire properly qualified and effective sales, support and engineering employees; • risks and expectations related to acquisitions and equity interests in private and public companies, including integration issues related to go-t

generation.costPolicy: Provider chain: Spark OAuth/free first, Gemini free second, approved DeepSeek v4 flash paid last on quota only.
